metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. International students face a hard grace-period deadline, not an open-ended window to weigh options. Detecting a company's applicant tracking system tells you whether to apply through a portal or contact a person. When no tracking system is detected, emailing the founder directly can beat the automated pipeline. The underlying pressure is AI-driven restructuring and cost cutting tied to quarterly earnings math.

artifactLines: International students face a hard grace-period deadline, not an open-ended window to weigh options.
Detecting a company's applicant tracking system tells you whether to apply through a portal or contact a person.
When no tracking system is detected, emailing the founder directly can beat the automated pipeline.
The underlying pressure is AI-driven restructuring and cost cutting tied to quarterly earnings math.

beats.narration_text: The 80-day countdown reframes the job search as survival, and the ATS-detection angle — "no ATS detected, email the founder directly" versus "uses Greenhouse" — is the tactical part I want to actually use. Given I have limited days, can you (1) explain how detecting a company's applicant tracking system should change whether I apply through the portal or reach a human; (2) list signals that a posting is real rather than a ghost listing; (3) map which outreach move fits each stretch of the 80 days?

output: She goes home planning interview questions
Monday morning, she’s handed two weeks notice
This isn’t a story about performance
